title: Inventory arsenic survey, testing-coverage, and health-outcome data sources for Bangladesh
outcome: A dated source inventory separating groundwater arsenic survey data (BGS/DPHE, national surveys), testing-coverage records (BAMWSP, national arsenic mitigation cell), health-outcome data, and population data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit reasons covering measurement method, geographic grain, temporal coverage, and accessibility.
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source and claims.json with data-sufficiency claim.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each source for URL, date, access status, measurement method documentation, geographic grain, and stated limitations. Reviewer verifies that the data-sufficiency claim kill condition is specific and falsifiable.
done_condition: At least six candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering arsenic measurement method, geographic grain, temporal coverage, testing-coverage documentation, and accessibility for sub-national analysis.

title: Define upazila-level arsenic exposure baseline from survey data
outcome: A reproducible baseline specification for upazila-level arsenic exposure estimates from BGS/DPHE survey data and subsequent national surveys, including well-depth stratification and denominator handling.
inputs: administrative boundary source for Bangladesh upazilas
expected_artifact: validation.md update with baseline formula, well-depth stratification, and data checks.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the baseline on a sample upazila and confirms well-level aggregation, depth stratification, and denominator behavior.
done_condition: A reviewer can reproduce the upazila-level exposure baseline calculation, identify every excluded well or upazila, and verify the depth stratification approach.

title: Map upazila-level testing-coverage gaps against known contamination
outcome: An upazila-level testing-coverage gap analysis combining known contamination zones (from survey data) with testing-coverage records (from BAMWSP and any current sources) and population data to identify where untested wells overlap with population density and prior contamination probability.
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with gap-analysis methodology and results for at least two upazilas.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the gap analysis with the documented methodology and confirms the upazila-level coverage and exposure-overlap calculations.
done_condition: Gap analysis includes at least two upazilas with documented methodology, testing-coverage estimates, exposure-overlap calculation, and replication status.

title: Red-team harmful interpretations of arsenic exposure and testing maps
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how exposure maps or testing-coverage gaps could stigmatise affected communities, discourage safe well use without alternatives, or misdirect testing resources.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every high-risk failure mode has a mitigation or blocks publication, then a second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every operational claim is either blocked, softened to analytic language, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether testing-coverage gap maps match field decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the proposed testing-coverage gap analysis would change resource allocation by the national arsenic mitigation programme or district-level public-health offices in Bangladesh.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for the national arsenic mitigation programme.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk for the output, then a second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user in the Bangladesh arsenic mitigation programme, named decision, explicit non-use cases, and replicated field-reality review before publication.
